gaps: A snapshot-completeness indicator (or refusal to fan out near-empty snapshots) so predictors know upfront the cell is base-rate-only
stats --group-by disposition for a pre-1925 / mandatory-jurisdiction slice specifically (a --date or era filter that works when date_filed is null)
helpful: fedcourts stats disposition base rates were the entire quantitative basis for this sparse-snapshot cell

notes: Snapshot for this docket carried no docket entries or dates, so corpus tooling did all the work. query output rows are very large (full summary HTML); a --brief mode would cut context cost.
